raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased an item from Best Buy online in the amount of {$480.00}, which was charged to my Best Buy credit card issued by Citibank. The item was shipped to my home. 

Best Buys published return policy at the time of purchase stated that online purchases could be returned by mail using a prepaid return label. That return option was important to my purchase decision. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Best Buy to initiate a return within the applicable return period. Best Buy confirmed in writing that they would not provide a prepaid shipping label and required that the item be returned in-store only. This restriction was not clearly disclosed in the published return policy for this item. 

Because I was unable to obtain a mail-in return option despite requesting it within the return window, I filed a dispute with Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, a provisional credit was issued. I provided documentation including the published return policy and the merchants written refusal to provide a return label. 

Citibank subsequently denied the dispute, first stating that the merchants return policy was not followed, and then issuing a final determination stating that no billing error occurred. The denial did not address the discrepancy between the advertised return terms and the merchants refusal to provide a mail-in return option. 

The dispute process extended beyond the merchants return window. As a result, the return period has now expired and I am no longer able to return the item. 

I am requesting review of whether the issuer properly evaluated the dispute, including whether advertised return terms that were relied upon at the time of purchase were honored.
